rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular bromocyclization of alpha-allenols to enantioenriched spirocyclic 2,5-dihydrofuran derivatives using KBr as the bromine source
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- to Br radical, which dimerizes to Br2 (and Br3-) as the active electrophilic brominating species; cathode concurrently reduces water to H2 and OH-

key_intermediates.name: bromonium ion intermediate (int. I) from allenol

electron_flow: At the anode, Br- is oxidized (two sequential peaks at ~1.0 and 1.4 V vs SCE) to Br•, which dimerizes to Br2 and equilibrates with Br3-; Br2 then electrophilically activates the allene of (R)-1h to a bromonium ion, which is opened by intramolecular anti-attack of the pendant hydroxyl followed by deprotonation to give the dihydrofuran. At the cathode, water is reduced to H2 + OH-.
rate_determining_step: Slow electrochemical release of Br2 (controlling Br2 concentration is responsible for the high yield vs direct Br2 addition)

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of KBr and substrate 1e
probe_reagents.observation: Two anodic peaks at ~1.0 and 1.4 V (Br-/Br• and Br3-/Br2); oxidative current increases when KBr + 1e are combined, indicating chemical reaction between electrogenerated Br2 and the allenol

probe_reagents.name: molecular Br2 (stoichiometric, direct or dropwise)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave product 2a in only 28% (direct) and 25% (dropwise) yield, much lower than the electrochemical conditions; KBr/Br2 combination gave 47%, supporting slow in situ Br2 release

probe_reagents.name: enantiopure (R)-1h and (S)-1h substrates
probe_reagents.observation: Chirality fully transferred (98% ee retained), consistent with a stereospecific bromonium-ion/anti-cyclization pathway rather than a free radical/racemizing route

notes: Chirality transfer with high ee retention argues for an ionic bromonium-ion mechanism (cation-initiated electrophilic cyclization) rather than a free Br-radical addition pathway; the role of electrochemistry is to slowly generate Br2/Br3- in situ rather than to perform direct SET on the substrate.
